description: Having or showing an accurate and deep understanding; perceiving the inner nature of things. 'Seloi' names the quality of penetrating vision—seeing past surface to structure, understanding what lies beneath.
sound_symbolism: The word sees through. 'Se' begins with sensing attention, perception reaching. Then 'lo' goes deep, penetrating below surface. Finally 'i' pierces to the core, insight complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'seloi wheo' (insightful elder). Related to 'thuni' (intuitive) but emphasizes depth of seeing rather than directness of knowing.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Insight (vipassana) sees things as they are—penetrating the three marks of impermanence, unsatisfactoriness, and non-self.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Insightful listening perceives what's beneath words—the feelings, needs, and meanings that speech often obscures.
